packing_list_date: Extract the packing list date. Labels: 'DATE' / 'Date' / 'Tarih'. Located in the header area near 'Packing List' / 'PACKING LIST' title or in the top-right corner. Examples: 'DATE: 16 APR, 2025', 'DATE: 23.01.2026', '26.03.2025'. If the date is split into day/month/year, join into one line without normalization. Add to packing_list_date.
invoice_no: Extract the invoice reference. Labels: 'Invoice No' / 'INVOICE NO.' / 'P.Code or Invoice N' / 'Commercial Invoice'. Examples: '25OIL-5', '260101MR01'. Some packing lists do NOT include an invoice reference — if absent, leave empty. Keep the number exactly as in the document (including symbols like N and hyphens). Add to invoice_no.
producer: Identify the producer. Labels: 'Producer' / 'Manufacturer' / 'MANUFACTURER, PACKER, SHIPPER' / 'PRODUCER FACTORY NO' / 'SHIPPER' (paired with company name). FALLBACK: when no explicit producer label exists, use the company name from the document HEADER (logo + company name at the top). Keep the full legal name (and address if present) in one line. Add to producer.
buyer: Identify the buyer/consignee. Labels: 'Buyer' / 'Customer' / 'Consignee' / 'To:' / 'RECEIVER' / 'THE BUYER AND THE ADDRESS'. Examples: 'To: OOO MOREODOR', 'RECEIVER: OOO MOREODOR', 'THE BUYER AND THE ADDRESS: LLC MOREODOR'. Keep the full name (and address if present) in one line. If absent, leave empty. Add to buyer.
seller: Identify the seller. Labels: 'Seller' / 'Supplier' / 'Shipper' / 'SHIPPER'. Keep the full name in one line. If absent, leave empty. Add to seller.
veterinary_seal: Extract veterinary/health seal numbers only when explicitly labeled. Labels: 'Veterinary seal' / 'Health seal'. DO NOT confuse with linear_seal (the linear/general seal labeled 'Seal No' / 'Seal Number'). If a veterinary seal number is shown, keep it as-is; otherwise leave empty. Add to veterinary_seal.
linear_seal: Extract linear seal number(s). Labels: 'Seal No' / 'Seal Number'. If multiple values (multiple containers), join with '; '. DO NOT confuse with veterinary_seal. Add to linear_seal.
country_of_origin: Extract country of origin. Labels: 'Country of origin' / 'Origin' / 'ORIGIN OF GOODS'. Must be an explicitly labeled field. DO NOT extract from shipping route phrases like 'From QINGDAO CHINA to VLADIVOSTOK RUSSIA' — those describe the route, not a labeled country of origin. If no explicit field, leave empty (do not infer). Add to country_of_origin.
products: The 'products' block is expandable: create product_1, product_2, ... in the order of item lines in the document. For each position fill all available fields. If an attribute is given once for multiple lines (e.g., one Latin species), duplicate it into the relevant product_i. In token_refs include references to the source text/table fragments used.
products[*].name_product: Extract the commercial name. Labels: 'Product' / 'Description of goods' / 'DESCRIPTION OF PRODUCTS' / 'DESCRIPTION OF THE GOODS' / 'Description'. Examples: 'FROZEN OILFISH HGT (LEPIDOCYBIUM FLAVOBRUNNEUM)', 'FROZEN RAINBOW TROUT WR', 'FROZEN SEAWEED SALAD (CHUKA SALAD)'. Join line breaks into one line. Add to products[i].name_product.
products[*].latin_name: Extract the Latin species name, typically in parentheses after the commercial name (e.g., 'ONCORHYNCHUS MYKISS', 'LEPIDOCYBIUM FLAVOBRUNNEUM', 'UNDARIA PINNATIFIDA'). Note: not every parenthesized text is Latin — '(CHUKA SALAD)' is a transliterated local name, not Latin. Pick only the scientific species name. If not shown, leave empty. Add to products[i].latin_name.
products[*].size_product: Extract size/grade. Examples: '1-3KG', '3-6KG', '0.8-1.2 KG', '1KG/BAG*10/CARTON IVP', '3-4 LB (1.3-1.8 KG)', '80/120'. Keep the source format and add to products[i].size_product.
products[*].unit_box: Extract the unit if explicitly shown. Labels: 'Unit' / 'per carton' / 'per kg' / 'CASE' / 'CTNS' / 'BAGS' / 'BOX' / 'CARTON' / 'BOXES'. If not shown, leave empty. Add to products[i].unit_box.
products[*].packages: Extract the number of packages. Labels: 'Cases' / 'Cartons' / 'Packages' / 'No. of packages' / 'Total BAGS' / 'NO. BOX' / 'Quantity/CTNS' / 'Quantity'. Examples: '40 / 80 / 542 / 491' (per-size BAGS), '738' (NO. BOX), '2,500' (Quantity/CTNS). If type is shown, keep it in one line (e.g., '1153 BAGS', '738 BOXES'). Add to products[i].packages.
products[*].net_weight: Extract net weight. Labels: 'Net Weight' / 'NET' / 'NET WEIGHT KGS' / 'N.W.(KGS)' / 'Net weight/KGS' / 'Вес нетто'. Keep number and unit (e.g., KG) in the original format (dot/comma). Examples: '913.70', '19,388.75', '25,000.00'. Add to products[i].net_weight.
products[*].net_weight_with_glaze: Extract net weight with glazing. Labels: 'Net Weight with Glazing' / 'NW with glazing' / 'NET WEIGHT WITH GLAZING KGS'. If absent, leave empty. Add to products[i].net_weight_with_glaze.
products[*].net_weight_with_ice: Extract net weight with ice. Labels: 'Net Weight with Ice'. If absent, leave empty. Add to products[i].net_weight_with_ice.
products[*].net_weight_with_glaze_and_pa: Extract a combined net weight value covering glazing and/or primary packing. Labels: 'Net Weight with Glazing and Primary Packing' / 'Net weight with primary package'. Note: this field may include only primary packing without glazing, or both — extract whatever combined value is shown. If absent, leave empty. Add to products[i].net_weight_with_glaze_and_pack.
products[*].gross_weight: Extract gross weight. Labels: 'Gross Weight' / 'G.W.' / 'G.W /KGS' / 'GROSS WEIGHT KGS' / 'Gross weight KGS'. Keep number and unit as in the document. Examples: '27677.94KGS', '20,623.35', '26,800.00'. Add to products[i].gross_weight.
products[*].price_per_unit: Extract unit price if shown. Most packing lists do NOT include prices — if absent, leave empty. Keep currency and original format. Add to products[i].price_per_unit.
products[*].total_price: Extract line total if shown. Most packing lists do NOT include amounts — if absent, leave empty (do not compute). Add to products[i].total_price.
products[*].factory_number: Extract the factory/establishment number. Labels: 'Establishment No' / 'Factory No' / 'PRODUCER FACTORY NO' / 'FACTORY NO.'. Format varies widely: numeric ('10751', '10735'), country prefix + number ('TR 61-0024'), or composite ('2100/02847', '3700/02C90'). Add to products[i].factory_number.
products[*].date_of_production: Extract production date. Labels: 'Date of production' / 'Production date' / 'PRODUCTION DATE'. May appear as a column in the goods table OR inside the description text (e.g., 'Date of production: 20.02.2025', 'Production date 05.01.2026'). If multiple dates (range), keep as in the source in one line. DO NOT confuse with packing_list_date or expiry date. Add to products[i].date_of_production.
products[*].HS_code: Extract HS/tariff code per item if shown. Format varies: 6 digits with dot ('0304.81'), 10 digits without separators ('0303899007'). May be inside the goods description or as a separate column. If absent, leave empty. Add to products[i].HS_code.
